message:
chore(beads): close ft-eu0no + file three fresh-eyes follow-ups (ft-220lj, ft-2xbhx, ft-bqfkr)

Four small bead-store updates. No code changes.

## ft-eu0no closed

"PolicyEngine rate limiter fresh-per-MCP-request makes rate_limit
config non-functional across all MCP tools" — originally opened
during policy.rs audit round 20, shipped a structural fix in
f540a5d5 but was left as `in_progress` because regression execution
was still pending.

Now verified and closed. The companion fixes in this push add:
- `mcp_mutation_rate_limit_is_shared_across_requests` promoted from
  `#[ignore]` to an active test,
- new `wa_send_rate_limit_is_shared_across_tool_instances`,
and `scripts/cargo-local.sh test -p frankenterm-core --lib
rate_limit_is_shared -- --nocapture` passes with 2 tests on the
local lane. Close reason records both tests + the lane that
exercises them.

## ft-220lj (new, closed in-flight)

"[security][LOW] ft session show emits raw cwd (sibling of ft-yj375)"
— filed by the post-session orchestrator fresh-eyes pass on 4d26269f.
Evidence points at `SessionShowPaneState.cwd` being copied straight
from the restored pane snapshot and serialized without a redactor
pass. Closed as of this push: `ft session show --pane` now routes
through `redact_session_show_pane_lookup_for_output` before both
structured and human rendering, and the regression test
`session_show_pane_lookup_redacts_sensitive_pane_fields` asserts the
redaction across cwd/command/title/session_id.

## ft-2xbhx (new, closed as false positive)

"[refactor][LOW] mcp_helpers::build_policy_engine is dead code after
ft-eu0no refactor" — fresh-eyes reviewer pointed at
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_helpers.rs:97` and claimed the
helper had zero callers. Closed as a false positive: the actual path
is `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:324`, where
`build_policy_engine` is called by
`build_policy_engine_with_shared_rate_limiter` at mcp.rs:353 as the
base helper. The reviewer agent hallucinated the path. That said,
the *mcp_helpers* copy — which genuinely had no callers — is
removed in a separate commit in this push to eliminate the
autocomplete landmine.

## ft-bqfkr (new, closed)

"[tx][race][P2] wa.tx_run: concurrent calls on same contract
double-execute side effects + race atomic-rename save" — filed
after b7b44081's synthetic-allow removal made the race exploitable.
Closed with the in-process-set + `fs2::FileExt::try_lock_exclusive`
advisory-file-lock combo in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`
plus the `<path>.<pid>.<nonce>.tmp` save-file uniqueness fix in
`crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_missions.rs`. Regression test
`tx_contract_lock_rejects_parallel_same_contract_execution` covers
the single-process case; the companion unique-tmp test
`tx_contract_tmp_paths_are_unique_per_save_attempt` covers the
cross-process case.

All four entries are now `closed`; no other beads are modified.

 {"id":"ft-26k3h","title":"[MEDIUM] survival::ActivityProfile::update_hour NaN-poisons hourly_ewma via clamp","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:455` — `ActivityProfile::update_hour`.

Same `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` gotcha as ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce. A NaN `normalized_activity`:

1. `sample = normalized_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` stays NaN.
2. First sample (sample_count == 0) path writes `self.hourly_ewma[index] = NaN`.
3. Any subsequent EWMA update is `alpha * NaN + (1 - alpha) * prev = NaN`.
4. Every `predict_hour` / `predict` call returns NaN for that hour slot, forever.
5. Downstream survival/hazard math using the predicted activity gets NaN — same silent-wrong-answer class as disk_pressure.

## Fix

Guard at entry: if `normalized_activity.is_nan()`, skip the update entirely. The slot keeps its last good value (or the default if uninitialised) and the next well-formed sample continues the EWMA.

## Tests

* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_does_not_poison_ewma` — update_hour(NaN) leaves hourly_ewma + sample_count unchanged.
* `ft_26k3h_update_hour_nan_then_clean_sample_still_tracks` — end-to-end: NaN → clean sample sequence still produces a finite, sensible EWMA value.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:50:37.797018Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.312035Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:53:29.311735Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-2guux","title":"reality-check gaps","description":"Reality-check sweep of the repo (pane 5, session 2026-04-20) against
README.md + AGENTS.md + docs/ft-xbnl0-verification-contract.md. Most
capability claims match the code; the four material gaps below are
worth tracking. No fixes this tick — documentation-only finding.

Methodology: read the README "Supported Surface Matrix" and "Quick
Example", then grep the CLI command tree at `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`,
the MCP tools at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs`, the web
endpoints at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/router.rs`, and the
runtime layer at `crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs` +
`runtime_compat.rs`.

Findings (severity ranked):

G1 [MEDIUM] Watch-daemon asupersync claim vs actual reactor dependency
  Evidence:
    - README.md:38-41 "runtime model is asupersync-native",
      "runtime_compat remains as an audited boundary".
    - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/runtime_labruntime.rs:13-18
      explicit comment:
        "Full ObservationRuntime integration tests (startup,
         shutdown, discovery, capture) require the runtime_compat::task
         module to be migrated from tokio::spawn to asupersync::spawn.
         Until that migration completes, ObservationRuntime::start()
         cannot run under asupersync RuntimeFixture because task::spawn
         → tokio::spawn panics without a tokio reactor."
    - runtime.rs:184 uses `crate::runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx`
      which still goes through the tokio-backed path when the
      `asupersync-runtime` feature is disabled.
  Why it matters: the primary operator surface (`ft watch`) cannot be
  exercised end-to-end under a pure asupersync runtime fixture today.
  The README is honest that runtime_compat is "an audited boundary";
  the specific gap is that the watch daemon's task spawns transit
  that boundary. Tracked by the ft-xbnl0.2.x epic but worth surfacing
  as a doc clarification on README.md:38-41 ("native with a named
  quarantine").

G2 [MEDIUM] `ft restart` is Unix-only, not disclosed in the support matrix
  Evidence:
    - README.md:124 "Session persistence | Supported with backend
      prerequisite | Snapshots, session inspection, restore, and
      `ft session doctor` are implemented".
    - crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:27032 emits:
        "ft restart is currently supported only on Unix platforms."
      and exits 1 on non-Unix; the full `execute_restart_workflow`
      call path sits inside `#[cfg(unix)]` at line 27036.
  Why it matters: a Windows operator following the README's
  session-persistence story will hit this only at runtime.
  Supportable via an asterisk in the matrix or an explicit
  "Unix-only" caveat in the `ft restart` section.

G3 [LOW] Performance claims lack linked artifacts
  Evidence:
    - README.md:51 "<50ms lag" for delta extraction.
    - README.md:62 "keeps 200+ panes under 1GB vs 4GB+".
    - README.md:113 "TOON reduces token consumption by 40-60%".
    - No matching benchmark artifact path in docs/ referenced from
      these claims; the verification-contract doc at docs/ft-xbnl0-
      verification-contract.md mandates Level C artifact bundles for
      soak/perf-adjacent claims but the README does not cite any.
  Why it matters: performance claims presented as current fact in
  the README should either cite an artifact bundle or be softened to
  "target" / "design goal" until evidence lands. Non-blocking.

G4 [LOW] `SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT = 200` silently bounds OSC133 state
  Evidence:
    - crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs:188
        `const SEND_OSC_SEGMENT_LIMIT: usize = 200;`
    - mcp.rs:288-310 `derive_osc_state_from_storage` walks only the
      last 200 segments. If OSC133 markers span further back, the
      derived state is silently incomplete; the helper returns
      `Ok(None)` with no gap marker.
  Why it matters: a quiet-pane edge case (no activity for >200
  segments) would yield an incorrect alt-screen / prompt state via
  the MCP surface without a visible warning. Worth either documenting
  the bound or recording a `markers_unknown_due_to_window_limit`
  signal.

Non-gaps verified present and matching claims:

  - `ft robot state` / `state --include-text`: main.rs:2375-2391
  - `ft robot search --mode {lexical,semantic,hybrid}`:
      main.rs:4633-4711 enum + From impl; semantic/hybrid properly
      gated behind `semantic-search`/`frankensearch` features.
  - `ft mcp serve`: main.rs:33124
  - `ft web` + `/stream/events` + `/stream/deltas`:
      web/router.rs:65,70 — streaming exists; README's
      "more than 'health only'" claim is accurate.
  - `ft distributed agent`: main.rs:165-168 under
      `#[cfg(feature = "distributed")]`.
  - `ft session doctor`, snapshot save/restore/list/inspect/diff:
      SnapshotCommands + SessionCommands enums defined around
      main.rs:4207.
  - `ft tx run`/`tx rollback`/`tx plan`/`tx show`: main.rs:21114+
      via `execute_tx_run_with_executor`.
  - Policy "21 subsystems": unified_telemetry.rs:242 comment; actual
      PolicyEngine struct at policy.rs:3466-3517 declares ~22
      subsystem-shaped fields. Close-enough; no drift worth fixing.
  - tokio/smol usage in supported core: grep finds zero production
      call sites outside `runtime_compat` + `dependency_eradication`
      + `forbidden_dep_guards` (guard files are intentional).

Documented only. No shippable fix this tick per /reality-check-for-project
ground rules. Related: AGENTS.md rule zero (NEVER delete
frankenterm-core) is intact; the four gaps above are additions to the
README, not subtractions from the code.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:01:41.726765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.915672Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:59.913552Z","close_reason":"Reopening-in-error by p5-orch robot-triage pick — the bead was already closed with the four reality-check gaps (G1 runtime_compat quarantine language, G2 ft restart Unix-only caveat, G3 softened perf/token claims, G4 OSC/OSC133 marker window bound) addressed across two commits:

1. 4c3952a9 docs: correct README claims vs actual (ft-2guux) — landed 2026-04-20, covers all four gaps:
   - G1: added 'The remaining watch-daemon/runtime startup path still crosses a named runtime_compat quarantine while the last task-spawn seams are being collapsed' to the Platform Model paragraph
   - G2: appended 'ft restart is currently Unix-only.' to the Session persistence row of the support matrix
   - G3: softened <50ms lag to 'target/benchmark lane'; softened 200+ pane memory envelope to 'benchmark-dependent until linked artifacts are published'; softened 40-60% TOON savings to 'lower-token machine format; savings vary by payload'
   - G4: added a new subsection '5.1 Marker Window Bound' documenting the bounded OSC/OSC133 marker derivation window

2. af925d26 docs(readme): correct runtime_compat boundary claim — landed 2026-04-21, further refined G1 language now that the 9e1bd63b cfg-strip has collapsed the dual-runtime fallback: 'The dual-runtime fallback is gone, but several production seams still route through the named runtime_compat boundary for runtime bootstrap, channels, time, blocking work, IPC, and mux interoperability while the remaining compat helpers are being collapsed.'

bv --robot-triage surfaced this bead as open-and-ready because the triage graph snapshot had not yet absorbed the prior close. Verified via git log --grep ft-2guux + direct README diff inspection: no work remains.  {"id":"ft-2oph2.2","title":"[ft-2oph2][perf] Reuse chunked scan buffer for definitive trigger scan","description":"# Goal
Eliminate duplicate full-buffer accumulation in the chunked scan pipeline when both trigger scanning and compression are enabled.

## Scope
- File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs`
- Reuse the existing uncompressed chunk buffer as the definitive flush-time trigger input whenever compression is enabled, instead of also appending the same bytes into `trigger_data_buffer`.
- Preserve exact batch/chunk trigger parity and compression behavior.
- Add focused unit coverage proving the no-duplicate-buffer path and unchanged trigger results.

## Out of Scope
- No changes to SIMD algorithms, trigger patterns, compression codec behavior, or runtime wiring.
- No changes to unrelated runtime/event/storage files.

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test proptest_scan_pipeline -- --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo bench -p frankenterm-core --bench scan_pipeline --no-run`

## Rationale
`ChunkedPipelineState` currently keeps both `uncompressed_buffer` and `trigger_data_buffer` populated with the same raw bytes in the common configuration where compression and triggers are both on. This inflates memory traffic and buffer growth on the hot path without changing behavior.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"TealLotus","created_at":"2026-03-31T18:02:43.603376Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917970Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T17:20:17.917717Z","close_reason":"Implemented chunked scan buffer reuse, strengthened compression-enabled trigger parity tests, and validated via rch-backed scan_pipeline/proptest/bench coverage; remaining repo-wide failures were unrelated.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-bkgbq","title":"[architecture][follow-up ft-zo3cr] Centralize PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in main.rs","description":"## Context

ft-zo3cr identified an architectural smell: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` repeats the PolicyEngine + PolicyGatedInjector + WorkflowRunner assembly in at least 4 branches with slightly-different defaults:

- :6992-6999 — PolicyEngine::new + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (bare, for a single policy check)
- :15341-15359 — PolicyEngine::permissive + PolicyGatedInjector + CxPolicyInjector + WorkflowEngine + PaneWorkflowLockManager (full workflow runtime stack)
- :19850-19869 — PolicyEngine::new(require_prompt_active) + with_tuning + with_command_gate_config + with_policy_rules (similar to :6992 but with injector+storage)
- :25526-25540 — PolicyEngine::new(true) + with_tuning BUT OMITS with_command_gate_config and with_policy_rules (drift!)

ft-zo3cr in-scope fix (landed in ddf4eb8f) closed a similar drift in mcp_tools.rs:2558. The main.rs sites were left out because this architectural bead scoped only crates/frankenterm-core/* via its reservation.

## Why this matters

The :19850 vs :25526 comparison is the exact kind of drift the bead warns about: one call site gates on command_gate + policy_rules, the other doesn't. Any future fix to one doesn't land in the other. The :19846-19849 comment in current code already records a past regression of this shape (workflow injector diverged from the direct send path).

## Suggested direction (matches the bead's original proposal)

Introduce a single composition factory in frankenterm-core (or a dedicated module under crates/frankenterm/src/) exposing shape-typed builders:

    fn build_human_send_stack(config: &Config) -> HumanSendStack { ... }
    fn build_mcp_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: StorageHandle, wezterm: ...) -> McpWorkflowStack { ... }
    fn build_robot_workflow_stack(config: &Config, storage: ...) -> RobotWorkflowStack { ... }

Each returns a bundle (PolicyEngine + Injector + optional Runner). Handlers at the 4 main.rs sites become declarative — they ask for the bundle rather than open-code the assembly.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Each of the 4 main.rs sites uses one of the shared factories.
- The feature set (tuning / command_gate / policy_rules / require_prompt_active) applied by each factory is asserted by a focused unit test (one per bundle shape).
- Any future config knob added to PolicyEngine has exactly ONE place to thread through.

## Scope note

Requires crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs reservation (not in ft-zo3cr's scope). Best picked up after any outstanding main.rs work (e.g. other rate-limiter or safety-config refactors) lands.

+{"id":"ft-bqfkr","title":"[tx][race][P2] wa.tx_run: concurrent calls on same contract double-execute side effects + race atomic-rename save","description":"Post-session fresh-eyes review of b7b44081 (ft-kbbc3 P1 fix) — fix is correct but introduces new exposure because steps now actually execute side effects.

Defect:
- mcp_tools.rs:3064-3204 load → execute → save path has NO tx-level lock
- Two parallel wa.tx_run invocations for the same contract can: (a) both send real SendText/WaitFor actions to the pane TWICE, (b) race the atomic-rename save in mcp_missions.rs:350 which uses a shared <path>.json.tmp filename

Before b7b44081: synthetic allow-all returned immediately, no side effects — race was theoretical
After b7b44081: steps fire real SendText/WaitFor, so concurrent calls now corrupt pane state + tx journal

Fix options:
1. Per-contract mutex keyed on contract_id (serialize at handler entry)
2. Make tmp filename unique (<path>.<pid>.<nonce>.json.tmp) then atomic-rename
3. Fail-fast if a concurrent tx_run is in flight for the same contract_id

Suggest option 1 + option 2 together. Add regression test: two spawn tasks calling wa.tx_run(contract) concurrently, assert exactly-once side-effect count + journal integrity.

-{"id":"ft-eu0no","title":"[review][HIGH] PolicyEngine rate limiter fresh-per-MCP-request makes rate_limit config non-functional across all MCP tools","description":"Proactive policy.rs audit round 20. policy.rs:2077-2088 defines RateLimiter with state HashMaps pane_counts and global_counts owned BY the PolicyEngine struct. mcp_helpers.rs:97-107 build_policy_engine returns a fresh PolicyEngine every call (no Arc, no shared Mutex). grep confirms build_policy_engine is called at mcp_tools.rs:1130/1514/2029/2272/2332/3187/3319 and at the NEW ft-x86z2 helper at :187 — every MCP tool invocation constructs a fresh engine with empty HashMaps.  {"id":"ft-eze48","title":"[fuzz] add FtviIndex::from_bytes harness for vector index loader","description":"The FTVI vector-index binary loader is public and currently unfuzzed.

Evidence:
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/mod.rs:73` publicly re-exports `FtviIndex`.
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/vector_index.rs:218-267` parses attacker-controlled bytes in `FtviIndex::from_bytes`, including magic/version checks, dimension/count headers, and per-record half-float decoding loops.
- `fuzz/Cargo.toml` has no target for `FtviIndex::from_bytes`.

Why it matters:
- This is a binary format loader over persisted search state, so malformed bytes can hit allocation pressure, unexpected EOF handling, half-float edge cases, and count/dimension loops.
- The surface is already public, synchronous, and easy to fuzz without extra seams.

Fix:
- Add a fuzz target that exercises both arbitrary raw bytes and structured valid FTVI buffers via `write_ftvi_vec` round-trips, asserting parse no-panics and round-trip sanity where applicable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":30,"created_at":"2026-04-23T19:26:37.331896Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.556262Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:44:04.555662Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 6567397b: add FtviIndex::from_bytes fuzz harness","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","review"]}

 {"id":"ft-siwlu","title":"[architecture] split read-path redaction out of policy.rs authorization surface","description":"Evidence:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:1-12 describes the module as the authorization layer, but the same file also owns Redactor at :3025-3037.
- Read-only serving surfaces now import policy.rs solely for sanitization: crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/handlers.rs:69-70, crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:955-967, crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:6866-6883.

Why this is a structural smell:
- Output redaction is a presentation/read-path concern, not an authorization decision.
- Coupling every read surface to policy.rs makes the policy module a grab-bag for both write authorization and response serialization hygiene.
- This raises regression risk: redaction fixes ship as policy changes, and policy refactors can accidentally break passive read surfaces.

Suggested direction:
- Extract Redactor into a dedicated sanitization/read_safety module with a narrow public API.
- Keep PolicyEngine / PolicyGatedInjector focused on authorize/approval/rate-limit semantics.
- Leave policy.rs depending on the new redaction module if needed, not vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:47:44.637975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:13:22.143478Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:13:22.143332Z","close_reason":"Completed: extracted Redactor into dedicated module with policy re-export","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","strategic"]}

 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean. Commit 09014aae.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
